lines: // By default, this is false (i.e. we will not create previews).
+	// WebhookDisabled disables webhook integration for this repository. When true,
+	// Grafana will not register or receive webhook events from GitHub and will poll
+	// the repository on an interval instead. Use this when Grafana is not reachable
// Path is the subdirectory for the Grafana data. If specified, Grafana will ignore anything that is outside this directory in the repository.
// This is usually something like `grafana/`. Trailing and leading slash are not required. They are always added when needed.
// The path is relative to the root of the repository, regardless of the leading slash.

+							Description: "WebhookDisabled disables webhook integration for this repository. When true, Grafana will not register or receive webhook events from GitHub and will poll the repository on an interval instead. Use this when Grafana is not reachable from the public internet.",

Description: "Path is the subdirectory for the Grafana data. If specified, Grafana will ignore anything that is outside this directory in the repository. This is usually something like `grafana/`. Trailing and leading slash are not required. They are always added when needed. The path is relative to the root of the repository, regardless of the leading slash.\n\nWhen specifying something like `grafana-`, we will not look for `grafana-*`; we will only look for files under the directory `/grafana-/`. That means `/grafana-example.json` would not be found.",
// Whether we should show dashboard previews for pull requests.
// By default, this is false (i.e. we will not create previews).
+	// WebhookDisabled disables webhook integration for this repository. When true,
+	// Grafana will not register or receive webhook events from GitHub and will poll
+	// the repository on an interval instead. Use this when Grafana is not reachable
// Path is the subdirectory for the Grafana data. If specified, Grafana will ignore anything that is outside this directory in the repository.
// This is usually something like `grafana/`. Trailing and leading slash are not required. They are always added when needed.
// The path is relative to the root of the repository, regardless of the leading slash.
+// WithWebhookDisabled sets the WebhookDisabled field in the declarative configuration to the given value
+// and returns the receiver, so that objects can be built by chaining "With" function invocations.
+// If called multiple times, the WebhookDisabled field is set to the value of the last call.
// WithPath sets the Path field in the declarative configuration to the given value
// and returns the receiver, so that objects can be built by chaining "With" function invocations.
// If called multiple times, the Path field is set to the value of the last call.
+	// Webhook integration is explicitly disabled for this repository, so polling will be
+	// used instead. Skip registration even if a webhook URL would otherwise be available.
+		logger.Debug("Skipping webhook setup: webhookDisabled is true")
logger.Debug("Skipping webhook setup as no webhooks are not configured")
+			name: "skip webhook setup when webhookDisabled is true",
+			// The builder must not be called when webhookDisabled is set, so no expectations.
name: "skip webhook setup when URL is empty",
@@ -50,6 +50,16 @@ func Validate(_ context.Context, obj runtime.Object, allowInsecure bool) field.E
+	// A custom webhook URL (spec.webhook) and webhookDisabled are mutually exclusive:
+	// one says "receive webhooks at this address" while the other says "never use webhooks."
+			"cannot be true when spec.webhook is set",
// Validate git-related fields (branch, path, token/connection) using the shared git validator
list = append(list, git.ValidateGitConfigFields(repo, gh.URL, gh.Branch, gh.Path, allowInsecure)...)
+			name: "webhookDisabled with spec.webhook set is invalid",
+			errorContains: []string{"webhookDisabled", "cannot be true when spec.webhook is set"},
+	// extra.Build never wraps a webhookDisabled repository in a GithubWebhookRepository,
+	// so reaching here with the flag set would be a bug. Guard anyway to be safe.
+		logging.FromContext(ctx).Warn("webhook hooks invoked while webhookDisabled is true; skipping")
+	// See OnCreate for the reasoning behind this guard.
+			name:      "no webhook when webhookDisabled is true",
+			name:      "no webhook update when webhookDisabled is true",
/** Whether we should show dashboard previews for pull requests. By default, this is false (i.e. we will not create previews). */

+  /** WebhookDisabled disables webhook integration for this repository. When true, Grafana will not register or receive webhook events from GitHub and will poll the repository on an interval instead. Use this when Grafana is not reachable from the public internet. */

/** Path is the subdirectory for the Grafana data. If specified, Grafana will ignore anything that is outside this directory in the repository. This is usually something like `grafana/`. Trailing and leading slash are not required. They are always added when needed. The path is relative to the root of the repository, regardless of the leading slash.
When specifying something like `grafana-`, we will not look for `grafana-*`; we will only look for files under the directory `/grafana-/`. That means `/grafana-example.json` would not be found. */
"description": "Whether we should show dashboard previews for pull requests. By default, this is false (i.e. we will not create previews).",
